project_overview.project_name: Climate Action Through Landscape Management Program for Results (CALM)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To increase adoption of sustainable land management practices and to expand access to secure land tenure in non-rangeland rural areas
project_overview.total_financing: US$1,675.7 million (IDA Grant: US$500 million, Government: US$1,175.7 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Rural communities in non-rangeland areas, with focus on women participation and vulnerable groups

primary_locations: All non-rangeland rural areas of Ethiopia
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - excludes predominantly rangeland areas (Afar and Somali Regions) due to ongoing civil disturbances and different physical/social characteristics

key_components.description: Field-based interventions to scale up participatory watershed management, establishment of Watershed User Associations (WsUAs), preparation of Watershed Management Plans (WMPs), capacity building, policy framework strengthening, and SLM research

Improved rural land tenure security through issuance of Second-Level Landholding Certificates (SLLCs), installation and operation of National Rural Land Administration Information System (NRLAIS), capacity building, and policy framework strengthening

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Agriculture (MoA) - lead agency; Natural Resource Management Directorate (NRMD) and Rural Land Administration and Use Directorate (RLAUD)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: National SLM Steering Committee chaired by State Minister for Natural Resources Management; Regional SLM steering and technical committees; RED&FS Platform
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: Decentralized procurement through federal, regional and woreda levels; estimated US$58.2 million in procurement activities (less than 3% of total program cost)
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: MoA overall responsibility; Central Statistical Agency (CSA) for independent verification; six-monthly and annual program reports; upgraded M&E systems with Management Information System (MIS)

identified_risks.risk_description: Civil disturbances and political instability could negatively impact implementation
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Careful planning of missions emphasizing security, strategic communication, sound safeguards monitoring, enhanced transparency and civil engagement

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited experience with PforR approach in MoA, significant regulatory framework revisions, challenge of implementing reliable M&E system
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Close World Bank support, annual work plans and budgets for SPG, coordination between development partners through RED&FS platform

identified_risks.risk_description: Inadequate operating budgets, IBEX connectivity issues, weak property management, procurement capacity limitations, fraud and corruption risks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Annual work plans and budgets, improved access to IBEX system, strengthened internal controls, procurement training, ethics and anti-corruption officer deployment

identified_risks.risk_description: Inadequate community participation, social pressure affecting voluntary choices, limited grievance mechanisms, potential forced labor risks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Training programs, environmental and social auditing, operationalization of ESMS at woreda level, independent third-party auditing, strengthened GRM

identified_risks.risk_description: Dependence on decentralized action at kebele and woreda levels, effective monitoring and independent verification challenges
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Annual work plans and budgets for SPG, use of experienced Independent Verification Agencies, technical assistance support

safeguards_and_social.environmental_category: Not specified in traditional IPF terms - PforR operation
triggered_safeguard_policies: Projects on International Waterways OP/BP 7.50 - No
Projects in Disputed Areas OP/BP 7.60 - No
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - National GRM established under ESPES Program with regional and woreda level structures; World Bank GRS and Inspection Panel available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Strong focus on women participation in WsUAs (minimum 30% in Executive Committees), joint land certificates for married couples, individual titles for female-headed households, women's access to extension services

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Program excludes Afar and Somali Regions due to ongoing civil disturbances and different rangeland characteristics; includes measures for strategic communication and enhanced transparency to address drivers of civil unrest
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia faces political instability with civil disturbances since 2018; land degradation and rural landlessness identified as drivers of unrest; program aims to address these underlying causes
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Careful planning of implementation support missions emphasizing security protocols; strategic communication and outreach to mitigate political risks
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: no - program focuses on existing rural communities in their current locations, though excludes predominantly rangeland pastoral areas
